sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Cisco Silicon One Breaks the 51.2 Tbps Barrier
feed.summary: Cisco has announced its fourth-generation Silicon One devices, the G200 and G202, three and a half years after the initial launch, demonstrating a rapid pace of innovation. The G200 is a 5 nm, 51.2 Tbps device focusing on enhanced Ethernet-based AI/ML and web-scale spine deployments, while the G202 offers similar benefits at 25.6 Tbps. These new devices leverage Cisco's high-speed ADC-based SerDes technology and a converged architecture to reduce costs, power consumption, and latency in web-scale and AI/ML networks.

feed.title: Supermicro Unveils MicroCloud, High-Density 3U 8 Node System Utilizing AMD Ryzen Zen 4 7000 Series Processors
feed.summary: Supermicro has launched a new MicroCloud server system, the AS -3015MR-H8TNR, which features high density with an 8-node, 3U design. This system utilizes AMD Ryzen Zen 4 7000 Series processors, optimized for server usage, offering high performance for applications like e-commerce, cloud gaming, and virtual private servers. It boasts PCIe 5.0 support, DDR5-5200 MHz memory, and up to 16 cores (32 threads) per CPU, providing a compact and efficient solution for data centers.

feed.title: Artificial Intelligence Opens a Generational Opportunity for Chipmakers
feed.summary: The article discusses how the expansion of AI applications, especially large language models, is creating a "generational opportunity" for chipmakers. It highlights the projected growth of AI chip spending to nearly $165 billion by the end of the decade, primarily benefiting GPU-based chip providers like Nvidia, and notes that the demand for specialized AI hardware is leading to significant investments, partnerships, and a potential "long capital investment cycle" for the semiconductor industry. The article also examines how this shift will impact traditional CPU providers and accelerate the replacement of legacy computing infrastructure with AI-first hardware.

feed.title: AMD, Broadcom stocks both drop for seventh straight session
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) and Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) experienced their seventh consecutive day of stock losses on Monday, marking their worst losing streaks since 2022. AMD shares fell 2.3% to close at $107.51, which is 33.6% below their record high from November 2021.

feed.title: Nvidia and AMD shares fall on report of new U.S. ban on AI chip exports to China
feed.summary: Shares of Nvidia Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. dropped following a report that the Biden administration is considering a new ban on sales of AI chips to China. This potential ban comes despite Nvidia managing to avoid $400 million in lost sales last year after a previous ban on some chip sales to China. The news impacted both companies with Nvidia shares falling 3% after hours and AMD shares also decreasing by 3%.

feed.title: Oracle is no longer buying Intel processors for its data centers
feed.summary: Oracle, one of the largest data center operators globally, has stopped purchasing Intel processors for its data centers. This decision was confirmed by Oracle Chairman Larry Ellison. Instead of Intel, Oracle is now buying billions of dollars worth of Nvidia and AMD chips.

feed.title: Chipmaker Micron beats on demand from booming AI, easing glut
feed.summary: Micron Technology exceeded third-quarter revenue estimates driven by high demand for its memory chips in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector and an easing supply glut in traditional markets. The company's shares rose over 2% after reporting stronger-than-expected earnings and anticipating improved pricing trends. Despite ongoing U.S.-China tech tensions impacting its presence in the Chinese market, Micron expressed confidence in the memory industry's recovery.

feed.title: Stock of the week - Advanced Micro Devices (29.06.2023)
feed.summary: This article discusses the potential impact of new US chip export restrictions to China on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). While such restrictions negatively affected semiconductor stocks, AMD's exposure may be limited as China's share of its revenue has significantly decreased. The article also compares AMD's stock performance to Nvidia and Intel, noting Nvidia's stronger recovery from the 2022 sell-off.

feed.title: Lockheed, GlobalFoundries Collaborate to Advance US Semiconductor Manufacturing
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin and GlobalFoundries (GF) are partnering to bolster U.S. semiconductor manufacturing, focusing on increasing the security, reliability, and resilience of domestic supply chains for national security systems. This collaboration, announced at GF's advanced manufacturing facility in New York, will leverage GF's technology to innovate microelectronics, including 3D heterogeneous integration, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride on silicon. The initiative aims to support the CHIPS and Science Act's goals by promoting onshore production and developing a chiplet ecosystem to produce chips more rapidly and affordably.

feed.title: iShares Taiwan ETF has a 16% dividend yield: Is EWT a safe buy?
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI Taiwan ETF (EWT) has seen a significant jump this year despite Taiwan's economy facing headwinds like geopolitical tensions and a semiconductor demand downturn, leading to a recession. The article suggests EWT is a good investment due to geopolitical fears creating discounts, an attractive dividend yield of over 16%, and supportive technical indicators, with major constituents in the tech industry like Taiwan Semi.

feed.title: ASE, Amkor, UMC and Samsung Getting a Slice of the CoWoS Market from AI Chips, Challenging TSMC
feed.summary: The surging demand for AI chips is overwhelming TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity, leading chipmakers like Nvidia to diversify their supply chains. This shift creates opportunities for other foundries and OSATs such as UMC, ASE (including SPIL), and Samsung, which are developing their own advanced packaging solutions to meet the growing market needs. Samsung, with its turnkey service and significant R&D investments in 2.5D packaging, is actively pursuing a larger share of the AI chip market.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. – More market share gains expected
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expected to gain further market share in the data center CPU market and capitalize on opportunities to expand its presence in the data center GPU market due to increased generative AI adoption. The company's margins are projected to grow post-acquisition of Xilinx and Pensando, driven by increasing revenue contributions from its Data Centre and Embedded segments. Phillip Securities Research initiates coverage with an Accumulate recommendation and a target price of US$125.00, anticipating marginal revenue growth in FY23e and re-acceleration in FY24e.

feed.title: Samsung, SK Hynix in next-generation HBM chip supremacy war
feed.summary: South Korean memory chip giants Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are locked in a fierce battle for supremacy in the next-generation High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) chip market, driven by the booming demand for generative AI technologies like ChatGPT. SK Hynix, a long-time leader in HBM, is showcasing its advanced HBM capabilities, including the world's first 12-layer HBM3 DRAM, while Samsung is aggressively increasing its HBM3 production capacity and aims for over 50% market share. Both companies view increased HBM sales as crucial for turning their semiconductor businesses profitable amidst significant operating losses.

feed.title: Semiconductor Stocks Are Hot -- So Is Their Long-Term Outlook
feed.summary: Semiconductor stocks and related ETFs, like the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH), have experienced significant growth this year, driven largely by enthusiasm for artificial intelligence, particularly due to companies like Nvidia. While concerns about valuation exist, analysts believe the long-term outlook remains attractive, bolstered by a rebounding PC and smartphone market, and the massive potential of AI development across industries.

feed.title: AMD EPYC CPUs Now Power SAP Applications Hosted on Google Cloud
feed.summary: AMD announced that SAP's cloud ERP delivery operations for RISE with SAP will now run on Google Cloud's N2D virtual machines powered by AMD EPYC processors. This integration aims to provide impressive performance, energy efficiency, and scalability for critical SAP Business Suite workloads. The collaboration focuses on helping enterprises modernize their data centers and accelerate time-to-value for their applications.

feed.title: Meet Indian-origin women on the 'America's Richest Self-Made
feed.summary: Four Indian-origin women, Jayshree Ullal, Neerja Sethi, Neha Narkhede, and Indra Nooyi, have been named on Forbes' list of America’s 100 richest self-made women, with a combined net worth of $4.06 billion. Each woman has achieved significant success in technology, IT consulting, or consumer goods, showcasing their entrepreneurial prowess and impact in their respective industries. The list calculates net worth using stock prices from May 12, 2023, and evaluations from external experts for private companies.

feed.title: Storage in space? The data's already there
feed.summary: As human activity expands into space, the need for data storage in otherworldly environments is growing, presenting unique challenges for vendors. Companies like HPE and Phison are developing specialized solid-state drives (SSDs) designed to withstand the harsh conditions of space, including radiation and extreme temperatures, while also mitigating issues like high latency for data processing. These space-hardened storage solutions are crucial for research on the ISS and future lunar bases, aiming to enable on-site data processing and reduce the cost and latency associated with transmitting large datasets back to Earth.

feed.title: Aehr Reports Record Revenue and Profit for Fiscal 2023 and Guides for Over 50% Increase in Revenue for Fiscal 2024 on Strength from Semiconductor Wafer Level Test and Burn-in Demand
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems announced record financial results for fiscal 2023, with net sales reaching $65.0 million and GAAP net income of $14.6 million, driven by demand for semiconductor wafer level test and burn-in products. The company also provided strong guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting revenue of at least $100 million, a 50% increase, and GAAP net income of at least $28 million, reflecting over 90% growth. This growth is attributed to increased adoption of their FOX systems and WaferPak Contactors for silicon carbide in electric vehicles, as well as new opportunities in silicon photonics and gallium nitride markets.

feed.title: Jayshree V Ullal rises on Forbes list of richest self-made women in America
feed.summary: Jayshree V Ullal, CEO and President of Arista Networks, has significantly climbed Forbes' list of richest self-made women in America with a net worth of $1.9 billion in 2022. Her success stems from transforming Arista Networks, taking it to IPO in 2014, and a distinguished career that included generating billions for Cisco, and holding key positions at AMD, IBM, and Hitachi. Ullal's journey highlights her leadership, expertise, and entrepreneurial spirit in the technology industry.

feed.title: Nvidia’s stock could have a pathway to $600, Citi says
feed.summary: Citi Research analyst Atif Malik has raised his price target for Nvidia Corp. stock to $520 from $420, citing the company's dominance in the rapidly growing artificial intelligence accelerator market. Malik also outlined a bull-case scenario where Nvidia's stock could reach $600. He anticipates blistering growth in the AI accelerator market, with Nvidia poised to outperform competitors.

feed.title: Xtrackers Semiconductor Select Equity ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the Xtrackers Semiconductor Select Equity ETF (CHPS), including its price, dividend and valuation, expense ratio, holdings, and performance metrics. It also offers insights into its ESG themes and scores, geographic exposure, and technical analysis, comparing it against peer ETFs. The ETF focuses on global semiconductors with an ESG screened index and has shown strong returns since its inception in July 2023.

feed.title: Microsoft and Meta expand their AI partnership with Llama 2 on Azure and Windows
feed.summary: Microsoft and Meta are expanding their AI partnership, bringing Meta's Llama 2 large language models to Azure and Windows. This collaboration aims to democratize AI by providing developers with open models, enabling them to build generative AI tools and experiences. Azure customers can now fine-tune and deploy Llama 2 models, while Windows developers can run Llama locally, integrating AI safety features from both companies.

feed.title: Samsung primed for graphics chip leadership with GDDR7
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics has unveiled the industry's first GDDR7 DRAM, which is faster and more energy-efficient than existing models. This new graphics memory product boasts a data transfer speed of 32 Gbps, making it able to process 50 ultra-high definition movies in one second, and consumes 20% less power than its predecessor. Samsung expects this technology to elevate user experiences in areas requiring outstanding graphics performance like AI, high-performance computing, and autonomous vehicles, positioning them to lead the graphics chip market.

feed.title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights GlobalFoundries, Lattice Semiconductor and Cirrus Logic
feed.summary: The Zacks Electronics – Semiconductors industry is experiencing growth due to the rising adoption of AI, Machine Learning, and Industry 4.0, along with strong demand for consumer electronics. Despite supply-chain challenges and geopolitical tensions, the industry is poised for continued expansion, driven by 5G deployment, smart device computing needs, and advanced packaging solutions. Zacks identifies GlobalFoundries, Lattice Semiconductor, and Cirrus Logic as promising stocks within this sector.

feed.title: Nvidia is ‘dominating’ and could unlock $300 billion in AI revenue by 2027, analyst says
feed.summary: Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh is increasingly optimistic about Nvidia's prospects, projecting the company could tap into $300 billion in AI-related revenue by 2027. Rakesh believes Nvidia will maintain a significant share in the AI server market despite potential future competition. This outlook comes as Nvidia's stock has already seen a 200% increase this year.

feed.title: Micron Delivers Industry's Fastest, Highest-Capacity HBM to Advance Generative AI Innovation
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced it has begun sampling the industry's first 8-high 24 GB HBM3 Gen2 memory, boasting bandwidth greater than 1.2 TB/s and pin speed over 9.2 Gb/s, a significant improvement over existing HBM3 solutions. This new memory offers a 2.5 times performance per watt improvement, setting new benchmarks for AI data center metrics and reducing training times for large language models like GPT-4. Micron's 12-high stack with 36 GB capacity is expected to sample in early 2024, further enhancing capacity and efficiency for generative AI and HPC applications.

feed.title: UMC forecasts utilization rate to fall to 65%
feed.summary: United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) anticipates its factory utilization rate will drop further to about 65% in the current quarter due to prolonged inventory correction and weak end-market demand. This represents the lowest utilization since Q4 2011, and the company finds it difficult to predict a bottom given customer inventory destocking through next quarter. Despite this, UMC plans to expand its 28-nanometer capacity and is doubling its silicon interposer wafer capacity to meet growing demand for AI chips.

feed.title: U.S. chipmaker AMD to invest $400 million in India by 2028
feed.summary: U.S. chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD.O) announced a significant investment of $400 million in India over the next five years, including the establishment of its largest design center in Bengaluru, creating 3,000 new engineering jobs. This move aligns with India's efforts to become a chipmaking hub, attracting other major players like Applied Materials and Micron with its incentive programs. AMD's expansion underscores the country's growing importance in the global semiconductor industry.

feed.title: AMD to establish $400M chip design center in India
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced plans to invest $400 million in a new chip research and engineering center in Bangalore, India, which will be its largest design center globally with 3,000 engineers. This expansion will bring AMD's total headcount in India to over 9,000 employees. The move follows similar investments by other major chipmakers like Micron Inc. and Applied Materials Inc. in India.

feed.title: AMD earnings preview: Is the company ceding ground to Intel?
feed.summary: This article previews AMD's upcoming earnings report, questioning whether the company is losing market share to Intel. Historically, Intel's struggles have benefited AMD, but Intel's recent performance suggests a potential shift. Wall Street will be looking for confirmation of this trend in AMD's latest quarterly results.

feed.title: Megatrends, PCs, Servers and Moore
feed.summary: This article from Citi Research outlines three megatrends shaping the IT Hardware and Networking space: Generative AI, 400G and 800G transition, and Digital Transformation. It also discusses the normalization of the PC market, the growth of the server and storage markets, and the significant projected growth for the semiconductor industry, driven by "More than Moore" advancements. The insights are based on reports from Citi Research analysts Atif Malik, Asiya Merchant, and Andrew Gardiner.

feed.title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 07/30/2023
feed.summary: This article details the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending July 30, 2023, highlighting that these funds are high-risk due to leverage. Key performers include China-focused ETFs like Direxion Daily FTSE China Bull 3X Shares (YINN) and Direxion Daily CSI China Internet Index Bull 2x Shares (CWEB), along with those tracking electric vehicles (EVAV) and specific tech giants like Meta (FBL) and Google (GGLL). The performance was driven by factors such as China's policy actions, growth in the EV sector, and strong tech earnings.

feed.title: Intel plans massive Oregon factory expansion, filings show
feed.summary: Intel plans a significant, multi-billion-dollar expansion of its research factories in Oregon, including a fourth phase for the D1X factory in Hillsboro and an overhaul of the D1A factory in Aloha, over the next five years. This investment, potentially one of the largest capital projects in Oregon's history, aims to strengthen the state's role in Intel's technology development and is supported by $90 million in state incentives. Despite current challenges for the chipmaker, the expansion is expected to create hundreds or thousands of new jobs, though it will also lead to increased air emissions.

feed.title: AMD stock rises 3% after earnings beat, CEO predicts ‘multiple winners’ in AI race
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) shares rose 2.7% after hours following an earnings beat, with its CEO predicting "multiple winners" in the AI race. The company's PC sales slightly exceeded estimates, while data-center sales were just under projections. This performance follows Intel Corp.'s recent report of a data-center beat and strong PC sales.

feed.title: Opinion: AMD’s AI era to begin near year’s end, but it’s looking more like a 2024 story
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. needs flawless execution for the launch of its first AI-targeted graphics chips. While AMD projected better-than-expected Q3 revenue, Wall Street is more focused on Q4, when the company plans to launch its first GPU chips for AI acceleration in data centers, suggesting its AI era will truly take off in 2024.

feed.title: Arm and Industry Leaders Launch Semiconductor Education Alliance to Address the Skills Shortage
feed.summary: Arm has announced the creation of the Semiconductor Education Alliance, a global initiative aimed at tackling the growing skills gap in the semiconductor industry. This alliance brings together key players from industry, academia, and government to develop accelerated educational pathways and resources, and to cultivate a diverse talent pipeline. The goal is to ensure the industry has the necessary workforce to support continued growth and innovation in semiconductor design, fabrication, and deployment.

feed.title: JP Morgan Maintains Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Neutral Recommendation
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained a Neutral recommendation for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), with an average one-year price target suggesting a 19.45% upside. The article highlights strong institutional ownership, projected revenue growth, and key financial holdings in the company. It also includes background information on AMD and its recent financial results for Q2 2023.

feed.title: phoenixNAP Powers Expanded AI and Cloud Services with Energy-saving, Ampere-Based Servers from Hewlett Packard Enterprise
authors: Hewlett Packard Enterprise via Business Wire
feed.summary: phoenixNAP, a global IT services provider, is expanding its Bare Metal Cloud platform by deploying cloud-native HPE ProLiant RL300 Gen11 servers across its 18 data centers. These servers utilize energy-efficient Ampere® Computing processors, enhancing performance and energy efficiency for AI inferencing, cloud gaming, and cloud-native workloads. This expansion addresses the growing demand for data-intensive workloads while mitigating the environmental impact of increasing energy consumption in data centers.

feed.title: Mobile chip giant Qualcomm shares slide amid worst smartphone slump in years
feed.summary: Qualcomm shares fell after the company gave a tepid sales forecast for the current quarter, indicating continued weak demand for mobile devices, especially in China which accounts for over 60% of its sales. The company plans further job cuts, having already recorded US$285 million in restructuring charges, as it takes a conservative view of the market amidst an industry downturn. This performance underperformed a broader chip industry rally, raising concerns about the smartphone market's recovery.

feed.title: AMD's stock breaks with chip selloff to finish week higher as analyst cites 'important buying opportunity'
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares unexpectedly rose last week, up 2.6%, despite a broader chip sector selloff that saw Nvidia and the PHLX Semiconductor Index decline significantly. An analyst pointed to this performance as an "important buying opportunity" for AMD. Nvidia's earnings report is anticipated on August 23rd.

feed.title: Micron Launches Memory Expansion Module Portfolio to Accelerate CXL 2.0 Adoption
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced the sample availability of its CZ120 memory expansion modules, designed to accelerate CXL 2.0 adoption. These modules, available in 128 GB and 256 GB capacities, enhance server systems by providing incremental memory capacity and bandwidth, particularly beneficial for AI, SaaS, and HPC workloads. Micron is collaborating with Intel and AMD to validate the CZ120 modules on their CXL-compatible platforms.

feed.title: BlackRock Inc. Acquires Additional Shares in Mercury Systems Inc.
feed.summary: BlackRock Inc. has increased its stake in Mercury Systems Inc. by acquiring an additional 100,009 shares, bringing its total holdings to 5,878,104 shares. This transaction highlights BlackRock's confidence in the aerospace and defense company, which is considered undervalued by GuruFocus's GF Valuation despite recent stock declines. Mercury Systems Inc. demonstrates good outperformance potential with a strong GF Score, profitability, and growth rank, but also presents some financial risks.

feed.title: The Case For Intel: An Illustration Of The ‘Value’ Investing Mindset
feed.summary: This article argues for Intel as a "value investment" due to its current undervaluation despite strong long-term prospects. It delves into strategic market trends, Intel's strong industry position, favorable political and geopolitical factors, and a revived company culture under CEO Pat Gelsinger. The author emphasizes that true "value investing" focuses on a company's ability to correct its problems and adapt to strategic environmental shifts over a multi-year horizon, rather than short-term financial details.

feed.title: Supermicro Announces High Volume Production of E3.S All-Flash Storage Portfolio with New CXL Memory Expansion Offerings
feed.summary: Supermicro is commencing high-volume production of its E3.S all-flash storage portfolio, which includes new CXL memory expansion offerings. These solutions support PCIe Gen5 drives and CXL memory modules to meet the demands of large AI Training and HPC clusters. The company's Petascale systems are a new class of storage servers designed for high-throughput, low-latency storage, available with Intel Xeon Scalable or AMD EPYC processors.

feed.title: Stan Druckenmiller's Duquesne adds GE, Builders FirstSource, exits AMD (NASDAQ:NWS)
feed.summary: Stan Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office significantly adjusted its portfolio, adding new stakes in General Electric, Builders FirstSource, PTC, Canadian Pacific Kansas City, and DoorDash. Concurrently, the firm exited its positions in Advanced Micro Devices, Bilibili, and Workday, and reduced holdings in several major tech companies. These moves indicate a shift in investment strategy, focusing on new sectors while divesting from previous large holdings.

feed.title: Tema Oncology ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:CANC)
feed.summary: The Tema Oncology ETF (CANC) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund focused on companies involved in cancer treatment and management globally. Launched on August 15, 2023, the ETF invests in pioneering companies using a bottom-up selection approach to identify attractive valuations. As of the article, it manages $170.52 million in assets and has seen a 6.8% increase in stock value since January 1, 2026.

feed.title: Insider Sell: AMD Chair, President & CEO Lisa Su Sells 75,000 Shares
feed.summary: Lisa Su, Chair, President & CEO of Advanced Micro Devices Inc (NASDAQ:AMD), sold 75,000 shares on August 15, 2023, as part of a broader trend of insider selling at AMD. Over the past year, Su has sold 500,000 shares with no purchases, and AMD's insider transaction history shows 25 insider sells and no buys. Despite this, AMD's stock is considered modestly undervalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.94.

feed.title: LX International liquidates Yantai LCD component warehouse in China
feed.summary: LX International has liquidated its Yantai LCD component warehouse in China due to decreased demand for LCD panels, particularly from key customer LG Display. This move follows LG Display's reduction in LCD module production at its Yantai plant as the market shifts from LCD to OLED technology for small panels. The liquidation reflects the evolving display market where OLED is expected to significantly increase its share in IT devices.

feed.title: TSMC’s CoWoS Dominance: Amkor, ASE, JCET’s Response
feed.summary: The article discusses how the increasing demand for high-performance computing, AI, and 5G has led to a shift towards advanced packaging techniques like 2.5D and 3D, exemplified by TSMC's CoWoS technology. Despite TSMC's significant role, traditional assembly and test firms such as ASE, Amkor, and JCET are actively developing their own advanced packaging solutions to remain competitive. These firms are securing partial orders and maintaining market share by catering to products not in the highest-end category, showcasing their continued relevance alongside wafer foundries.

feed.title: Samsung weighs retooling Galaxy S series with Exynos chipsets
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics is considering reintroducing its Exynos chipsets into some Galaxy S models next year to reduce its reliance on Qualcomm's Snapdragon chips, which have led to rising application processor (AP) purchasing costs. The company spent \$4.3 billion on APs in the first half of this year, and aims to regain bargaining power lost by exclusively using Qualcomm chips in its latest premium phones. Samsung is reportedly looking at a dual-chip strategy for the Galaxy S24 series, with Exynos chips potentially used in some variants.

feed.title: Why Ireland is well positioned to capitalise on chip ‘gold rush’
feed.summary: Ireland is well-positioned to capitalize on the booming semiconductor industry, which is projected to double by 2030, thanks to its long-standing presence of major players like Analog Devices and Intel. However, the country faces challenges including intense global competition for investment, capacity constraints for manufacturing, and a scarcity of electronic engineering talent. To maintain its competitive edge, Ireland must continue to invest in R&D, upgrade its infrastructure, and foster international collaboration within the semiconductor ecosystem.

feed.title: GraniteShares' Single Stock ETFs Will Follow Nvidia, Tesla, AMD
feed.summary: GraniteShares has launched four new single-stock ETFs to capitalize on the trading momentum of Nvidia, Tesla, and Advanced Micro Devices. These new ETFs offer unique leveraged and short exposures, with the GraniteShares 1.5x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NVD) launching just before Nvidia's earnings report. The asset manager plans to roll out almost three dozen more such filings, emphasizing being first to market with these trading-focused products.

feed.title: AMD debuts two new graphics cards for the consumer market
feed.summary: AMD has introduced two new graphics cards, the Radeon RX 7800 XT and RX 7700 XT, to compete with Nvidia in the consumer chip market. These cards, priced at $499 and $449 respectively, are positioned as more affordable alternatives to high-end GPUs while offering significant performance per dollar. Based on AMD's latest RDNA 3 architecture, they include features like upgraded machine learning accelerators, ray tracing optimization, and FSR 3 technology for improved gaming visuals.

feed.title: New AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT and Radeon RX 7700 XT Graphics Cards Deliver High-Performance, Visually Stunning 1440p Gaming Experiences and Superior Performance-Per-Dollar
feed.summary: AMD has introduced new Radeon RX 7800 XT and RX 7700 XT graphics cards, designed to provide high-performance 1440p gaming experiences with superior performance-per-dollar. These cards, built on RDNA 3 architecture, offer 16GB and 12GB of GDDR6 VRAM respectively, and support features like AMD HYPR-RX and the new FidelityFX Super Resolution 3 for enhanced framerates and visual fidelity. The products are expected to be available from September 6, 2023.

feed.title: Unusual Call Option Trade in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Worth $1,281.25K
feed.summary: An unusually large call option trade worth $1,281.25K was made for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) on August 28, 2023, with a strike price of $115.00 and expiring on October 20, 2023. This trade is significantly above the mean for AMD options. The article also provides details on fund sentiment, analyst price forecasts, and other significant shareholders.

feed.title: Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:IGPT)
feed.summary: The Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT) invests in information technology equity, focusing on companies that derive over 50% of their revenue from the future media industry. As of April 2026, IGPT was trading at $71.12, showing a 19.6% increase year-to-date, with major holdings including Advanced Micro Devices, Alphabet, and NVIDIA. The ETF has current assets under management of $789.78 million and an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy."

feed.title: Coherent to set up centre of excellence at IIT-Madras Research Park
feed.summary: American firm Coherent has signed a tripartite memorandum of understanding with Guidance Tamil Nadu and IIT-Madras Research Park (IITMRP) to establish its first global Centre of Excellence (CoE) in India on the IITMRP campus. This CoE will focus on R&D for lasers, optical networking components, EV batteries, and compound semiconductor devices. The company plans to hire technical talent and start with an applications engineering lab for laser materials processing, leveraging Tamil Nadu's innovation ecosystem and engineering talent.

feed.title: Nvidia Says It Will Invest In Arm’s IPO After Failed Acquisition
feed.summary: Nvidia has confirmed its intention to invest in Arm's initial public offering, despite its failed acquisition attempt of the company. The GPU giant was named as a potential "cornerstone investor" in Arm's updated IPO filing, alongside other tech companies like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Google. This investment highlights the continued importance of Arm's architecture to Nvidia's products and the broader tech ecosystem.

feed.title: Hyperscalers have been choosing Arista over Cisco for data center switches
feed.summary: For the first time in years, enterprise spending, not cloud service provider spending, drove the growth of the data center switch market in Q2 2023, according to Dell'Oro Group. Cisco and Arista Networks remain the leading vendors, with Arista excelling in cloud service providers and Cisco in enterprise. The report also highlights that while AI investments are shifting budgets from traditional front-end networks, the Ethernet switch market continues to grow, driven by easing supply chain constraints and increased demand for higher-speed switches from hyperscalers.

feed.title: POET Technologies’ New Module Designs Attract Major Companies at CIOE
feed.summary: POET Technologies' joint exhibition with Super Photonics Xiamen (SPX) at CIOE in Shenzhen was a significant success, leading to numerous customer engagements and requests for samples of their 800G and 400G optical engines. The company's new module designs, based on the POET Optical Interposer, simplify assembly by eliminating the need for active alignments and attracted major end-users like Alibaba, FiberHome, and ByteDance. The positive reception at the event signals strong near-term commercial activity for POET.

feed.title: Arm climbs 25% in Nasdaq debut after pricing IPO at $51 a share
feed.summary: Arm Holdings saw its stock jump nearly 25% on its first day of trading on the Nasdaq after pricing its IPO at $51 per share, valuing the company at almost $60 billion. SoftBank, which took Arm private in 2016, retains about 90% of the company's outstanding shares. Despite its high valuation relative to its current growth, Arm's unique royalty-based business model, including revenue from decades-old designs, and its strategic importance in AI and chip design, are key factors in investor interest.

feed.title: Direxion Daily NVDA Bull 2X Shares ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:NVDU)
feed.summary: The Direxion Daily NVDA Bull 2X Shares ETF (NVDU) provides 2x leveraged exposure to the daily price movement of NVIDIA stock. Launched on September 13, 2023, the ETF has $697.94 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.97%. As of May 22, 2026, NVDU was trading at $142.38, reflecting a 20.9% increase since the beginning of the year.

feed.title: Chip Stocks Drop After Report Says TSMC Is Worried About Demand
feed.summary: Chip stocks dropped following a report that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), the world's largest third-party semiconductor manufacturer, has expressed concerns about slowing chip demand. TSMC reportedly told its suppliers to delay some deliveries, indicating potential weakness in the broader chip market. This news directly impacted investor sentiment towards the technology sector.
